Article.ArticleTitle: Low-load resistance exercise with and without blood flow restriction: Which is more effective for increasing local muscle endurance and why?

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: Department of Health and Rehabilitation, Institute of Neuroscience and Physiology, Sahlgrenska Academy, University of Gothenburg, Gothenburg, Sweden.

Reference.Citation: Bakkman, L. , Sahlin, K. , Holmberg, H. C. , & Tonkonogi, M. (2007). Quantitative and qualitative adaptation of human skeletal muscle mitochondria to hypoxic compared with normoxic training at the same relative work rate. Acta Physiologica, 190(3), 243–251.

Farup, J. , De Paoli, F. , Bjerg, K. , Riis, S. , Ringgard, S. , & Vissing, K. (2015). Blood flow restricted and traditional resistance training performed to fatigue produce equal muscle hypertrophy. Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports, 25(6), 754–763.

Groennebaek, T. , Jespersen, N. R. , Jakobsgaard, J. E. , Sieljacks, P. , Wang, J. , Rindom, E. , Musci, R. V. , Bøtker, H. E. , Hamilton, K. L. , Miller, B. F. , de Paoli, F. V. , & Vissing, K. (2018). Skeletal muscle mitochondrial protein synthesis and respiration increase with low‐load blood flow restricted as well as high‐load resistance training. Frontiers in Physiology, 9, 1796.

Ida, A. , & Sasaki, K. (2024). Distinct adaptations of muscle endurance but not strength or hypertrophy to low‐load resistance training with and without blood flow restriction. Experimental Physiology, 109(6), 926–938.

Petrick, H. L. , Pignanelli, C. , Barbeau, P. A. , Churchward‐Venne, T. A. , Dennis, K. M. , van Loon, L. J. , Burr, J. F. , Goossens, G. H. , & Holloway, G. P. (2019). Blood flow restricted resistance exercise and reductions in oxygen tension attenuate mitochondrial H2O2 emission rates in human skeletal muscle. The Journal of Physiology, 597(15), 3985–3997.

Pignanelli, C. , Petrick, H. L. , Keyvani, F. , Heigenhauser, G. J. , Quadrilatero, J. , Holloway, G. P. , & Burr, J. F. (2020). Low‐load resistance training to task failure with and without blood flow restriction: Muscular functional and structural adaptations. American Journal of Physiology‐Regulatory, Integrative and Comparative Physiology, 318(2), R284–R295.

Wernbom, M. , Augustsson, J. , & Raastad, T. (2008). Ischemic strength training: A low‐load alternative to heavy resistance exercise? Scandinavian Journal of Medicine & Science in Sports, 18(4), 401–416.
